positive_experience.situation: When a patient presents with redness, swelling, and pus around a nail, and the history suggests recent trauma or nail-biting, but no imaging or lab results are available, what should the doctor do next?

current_turn_information: Tool output: Document 1: Complications of liver dialysis include hemorrhage, secondary infections, hypotension.
Tool output: Document 2: Transjugular intrahepatic portosystemic shunts (TIPS) decompress portal hypertension and may improve renal function.

Tool output: Document 3: Indications for liver dialysis include acute liver failure, acute on chronic liver failure, paracetamol overdose, decompensated cirrhosis, Budd Chiari syndrome, endotoxemia, fulminant hepatic failure.
Tool output: Document 4: TIPS procedure-related morbidity/mortality: fatal complications 1.7% (range 0.6-4.3%), inversely related to procedure volume.

Tool output: Document 5: Main complications: bleeding, infection, heart failure, liver failure, kidney failure, fever, vascular/bile duct damage (<5%); hepatic encephalopathy affects one-third of patients.
case_memory.prior_information_summary: 37-year-old female with ESRD on HD due to IgA nephropathy, schizoaffective disorder, depression, anemia, GERD, hypothyroidism, RLE DVT, esophagitis. History of multiple failed access procedures, s/p R brachial artery to RA AVG (thrombosed), s/p R transhepatic HD catheter placement (replaced 6x), s/p L iliac stent, s/p ex lap and LOA for SBO. Self-mutilating disorder pulling out catheter. Recent seizures (negative for intracranial bleed, no anti-seizure meds). New t-wave inversions on EKG, mild troponin bump (21), no chest pain. PE: chronically ill, R flank catheter site non-bleeding, no drainage/erythema. Fluoroscopic replacement of transhepatic catheter via existing tract, tip in right atrium, no immediate complications. Final result: Fitting and adjustment of extracorporeal dialysis catheter.

case_memory.prior_information_summary: 37-year-old female with ESRD on HD from IgA nephropathy, schizoaffective disorder, depression, anemia, GERD, hypothyroidism, RLE DVT, esophagitis. History of multiple failed dialysis access procedures, including R brachial artery to RA AVG (thrombosed), R transhepatic HD catheter placement (replaced 6 times last year). Self-mutilating disorder causing catheter removal. Recent t-wave inversions, mild troponin bump (21), no chest pain. Negative head CT for intracranial bleed. No anti-seizure meds. Physical exam: R flank catheter site non-bleeding, no drainage, no erythema. Fluoroscopic replacement of transhepatic catheter via existing tract; catheter tip in right atrium, no immediate complications. Final result: Fitting and adjustment of extracorporeal dialysis catheter.
